steps.title: Read the given relationship
steps.content: The original equation is $$11m = 9(n+p).$$ The goal is to isolate $m$ on one side so the result expresses $m$ purely in terms of $n$ and $p$.

steps.title: Divide both sides by 11
steps.content:
Since 11 multiplies $m$, divide both sides by 11:
$$\frac{11m}{11} = \frac{9(n+p)}{11}$$
$$m = \frac{9(n+p)}{11}.$$

steps.title: Evaluate the answer choices
steps.content:
• A. $m=\frac{9(n+p)}{11}$ — exactly what algebra produced.
  • B. $m=9(n+p)$ — drops the division by 11.
  • C. $m=9(n+p)-11$ — subtracts 11 instead of dividing.
  • D. $m=9-n-p-11$ — not algebraically equivalent.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
